title: QUIC Server-Role Managed TLS Crypto Floor Verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests and inspection evidence for the narrow server-role ClientHello to ServerHello crypto floor behind the existing transcript-progress and bridge-driver seam.
expected_result: The server role accepts only the supported ClientHello subset for TLS 1.3 with TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 over secp256r1, preserves staged peer transport parameters from that ClientHello, constructs one local ServerHello, exposes those bytes through the existing Handshake crypto-output seam, publishes explicit Handshake open/protect material and Handshake key availability only after the ClientHello plus ServerHello transcript boundary exists, rejects malformed or unsupported ClientHello progression deterministically, and still leaves server-role commit and wider handshake support unavailable.
procedure: Run the CRT requirement-home tests that cover the supported server-role ClientHello path, outbound ServerHello exposure, Handshake key-availability publication ordering, malformed framing rejection, unsupported version or cipher-suite rejection, unsupported named-group or key-share rejection, and repeated or conflicting progression rejection.

Run the RFC9001 requirement-home tests that still cover outbound Handshake packet emission, Handshake packet protection, transport-parameter staging, and deterministic rejection of malformed Handshake packet input on the bridge seam.

Inspect the preserved server-local quic-go handshake rerun at `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-110409619-server-nginx/` together with the captured replay in `tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0112.cs` to confirm the remaining live failure is a current-floor rejection of unsupported key-share input rather than a listener startup or report-capture issue.

Inspect the bridge driver, transcript-progress owner, and key-schedule implementation to confirm the slice stays inside the main library seam, keeps runtime or host wiring unchanged, keeps commit unavailable, and does not add native TLS fallback.
scope: This slice verifies only the permanent server-role crypto floor from peer ClientHello through local ServerHello and Handshake packet-protection material availability. It does not prove EncryptedExtensions, certificate flight, server Finished, client-certificate authentication, 0-RTT, 1-RTT, key update, endpoint-host wiring, or interop-runner handshake support.
preconditions: The tests project can access internal library seams through the existing friend assembly relationship.
status_summary: Passed on 2026-04-22; focused requirement-home tests prove the supported server-role ClientHello to ServerHello floor, Handshake key-publication ordering, deterministic malformed-input rejection, and the intentional rejection of unsupported named-group or key-share input under the current `TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256` over `secp256r1` subset. The preserved server-local quic-go handshake rerun at `artifacts/interop-runner/20260422-110409619-server-nginx/` shows the listener now reaches the live handshake path, and the captured ClientHello replay from the preserved qlog demonstrates that the remaining peer offer carries key shares `0x11EC` and `0x001D`, which stays outside the current traced floor rather than indicating a leftover startup bug.
